label: yes
cot_prefix: Okay, let's try to figure out this problem. So Jane was born on the last day of February in 2000. Today is her 16th birthday. We need to find the date one week from today in MM/DD/YYYY format. The options are given, so maybe we can check each one. First, the last day of February is usually the 29th, right? But wait, February 2000... Let me think. 2000 is a leap year? Hmm, leap years are divisible by 4, but if they're divisible by 100, they must also be divisible by 400. So 2000 divided by 400 is 5, so yes, 2000 was a leap year. So Jane was born on 2/29/2000. Now, today is her 16th birthday. So 16 years after 2000 is 2016. But wait, does that mean today is 2/29/2016? But 2016 is also a leap year, right? Because 2016 divided by 4 is 504, no remainder. So yes, 2016 has a February 29th. So her 16th birthday would be on 2/29/2016. But wait, the problem says "today is her 16-year-old birthday," so today must be that date. But then the question asks for the date one week from today. So adding 7 days to 2/29/2016. Let's count. February has 29 days that year. So adding 7 days: 2/29 + 1 day is March 1st. Then adding 6 more days: March 1 + 6 days is March 7th. So one week later would be March 7, 2016. So the date would be 03/07/2016. Let me check the options. Option A is 03/07/2016. But let me check if I made any mistake here. Alternatively, maybe there's a trick. For example, in some non-leap years, if someone is born on February 29th, their birthday is considered to be February 28th or March 1st. But the problem says today is her 16th birthday. Since 2016 is a leap year, her actual birthday would exist, so today is 2/29/2016. Adding a week would be March 7th. So option A. Looking at the options: A is 03/07/2016. But let me check if maybe the problem is in another aspect. For example, is there a possibility that the birthday is celebrated on March 1st in non-leap years? But since 2016 is a leap year, she would have her actual birthday on 2/29. Therefore, one week from that date would be March 7. So the answer should be A. Let me check the other options. Option D is 03/06/2016. If I miscalculated adding days... Let's recount: Starting from
